votes.reason: The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification by counting the number of mismatched elements between the first half of the array and its reversed version, and checking if it equals cnt.
The Python code correctly implements the logic of counting mismatches between the first half of the array and its reverse, matching the Coq specification's use of fold_left and seq.
The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification by counting the number of mismatched elements between the first half of the array and its reversed version, and checking if it equals cnt.

Both implementations count the number of positions i in [0, len(arr)//2) where arr[i] differs from arr_reversed[i]. The Coq code uses fold_left with a conditional increment, and the Python code uses a for loop with the same logic. Both return/check whether cnt equals this count.

Both implementations count the number of positions i in [0, len(arr)//2) where arr[i] differs from arr_reversed[i]. The Coq code uses fold_left with a conditional increment, and the Python code uses a for loop with the same logic. Both return/check whether cnt equals this count.

Both implementations count the number of positions i in the first half of the array where arr[i] differs from arr[len-1-i]. The Coq code uses fold_left over seq 0 (length arr / 2) with nth i arr_reversed 0 which equals arr[len-1-i], and the Python code iterates over range(len(arr) // 2) comparing arr[i] with arr_reversed[i] which is also arr[len-1-i]. Both return whether cnt equals this count.
Both compute the count of mismatches between arr[i] and the reversed array at index i for i in 0..len(arr)//2-1, and check cnt equals this count. Matches for all lengths and values.
Both compute the number of mismatched symmetric pairs over indices 0..len(arr)//2-1 using the reversed list and check cnt equals that count; division, reversal, and comparisons align.

Both compute the number of mismatches between arr[i] and the mirrored element arr[len-1-i] for i in 0..len(arr)//2-1, and check cnt equals that count. Reversal, indexing, and integer division semantics match.
